task_completion.description: Agent opens and reads the incident report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal displaying the contents of 'post_incident_investigation.txt'.

task_completion.description: Agent accesses the CAMEO Chemicals database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser open to the CAMEO Chemicals website.

task_completion.description: Agent uses CAMEO Chemicals to check the compatibility of Toluene with the other chemicals.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAMEO Chemicals interface showing search results, chemical data sheets, or a compatibility report for Toluene, Nitric Acid, Hydrogen Peroxide, or Potassium Permanganate.

task_completion.description: Agent writes the correct root cause and chemical mechanism in the output report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing the output report file (either 'incident_root_cause_report.txt' or 'incident_root_cause_analysis.txt') containing an explanation that Toluene and Nitric Acid react to form explosive nitrotoluenes (e.g., TNT) via a nitration reaction.

task_completion.description: Agent evaluates the alternative cleaning agents in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor showing the report stating that both Hydrogen Peroxide (H2O2) and Potassium Permanganate (KMnO4) are strong oxidizers and are incompatible with Toluene residues.

task_completion.description: Agent includes future preventive measures in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text editor showing the report includes recommendations such as completely purging/degassing the reactor or using inert solvents before introducing oxidizing cleaning agents.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used the CAMEO Chemicals web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating the CAMEO Chemicals website in a web browser, rather than hallucinating facts or using an unauthorized script.

integrity.description: Verify the agent wrote the report to the specified file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen editing a file named 'incident_root_cause_report.txt' (or 'incident_root_cause_analysis.txt') in a text editor, rather than just printing the final answer to the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct root cause is the nitration of toluene residues by concentrated (68%) nitric acid, forming explosive nitrotoluenes (like TNT) under heat. The alternative agents, 30-35% Hydrogen Peroxide and Potassium Permanganate, are both strong oxidizers and highly incompatible with toluene/organic residues, posing similar explosion/oxidation hazards. Preventive measures should include completely purging/degassing the reactor and using inert solvents before introducing oxidizers. Note: The output file might be named 'incident_root_cause_report.txt' or 'incident_root_cause_analysis.txt'.
